title: MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: INTERMOUNTAIN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Migrant Education is a federal program that provides funds to help state and local educational agencies remove barriers to migrant students' school enrollment, attendance, and achievement. These services can include tutoring, individual help in class or after school, summer school, free 24-hour accident injury insurance, parent involvement support, referrals to community resources, and college and career transition support for older youth. 2001 SW Nye Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following criteria: -Age 21 and younger -Eligible for free public education through grade 12 and who have not yet graduated or obtained a GED -Moved in the preceding 36 months due to economic necessity from one residence to another and from one school district to another OR to accompany or join a parent, guardian, or spouse who is a migratory agricultural worker or migratory fisher -Must have engaged in new qualifying temporary or seasonal work in agriculture or fishing within 60 days of the move -Must have employment of less than 12 months duration in agricultural or fishing industries Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 966-3163 ..12.. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 966-3163 Eric Volger [email protected] Intake (541) 561-4922 Student Records Clerk Obdulia Munoz [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: A certificate of eligibility will be filled out in order for students to receive services LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Morrow and Umatilla Counties Services: Migrant Education Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Migrants Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - SPECIAL EDUCATION EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - HOME SCHOOLING INFORMATION SERVICES - BLUE MOUNTAIN EARLY LEARNING HUB PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - PRESCHOOL PROMISE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. INTERMOUNTAIN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT
